title: Food and meals
objectives: Talk about the three daily meals

vocab.hint: comida = lunch in Spain, almuerzo more common in LatAm

grammar_notes: Many uncountable foods take definite articles where English uses none: 'Me gusta el café' = 'I like coffee'.
Spain uses 'zumo' and 'comida' (for lunch). Latin America uses 'jugo' and 'almuerzo'. Both are correct.
example_phrases: Para desayunar, tomo café y pan. (For breakfast, I have coffee and bread.)
No como carne. (I don't eat meat.)
